name: akcli
description: AI-native KiCad design agent (zero-dependency Python CLI + Claude Code plugin): author and edit .kicad_sch from JSON op-lists with net-diff safety rails, run ERC/design/intent/BOM checks, simulate on KiCad's bundled ngspice, source real parts and fetch datasheets, and import Altium .SchDoc/.SchLib/.PcbDoc — no Altium or KiCad install required.

interface.shortDescription: Author & edit KiCad .kicad_sch from op-lists, run ERC/design checks, simulate on bundled ngspice, and import Altium schematics — no EDA install required.
interface.longDescription: Drives the zero-dependency `akcli` CLI: parse Altium .SchDoc/.SchLib/.PcbDoc and KiCad .kicad_sch/.kicad_sym/.kicad_pcb into one normalized model, extract netlists, run ERC-lite / power-rail / BOM / pinmap / revision-diff checks, author and apply KiCad schematics from an op-list, and source real JLCPCB/LCSC parts. Bundles twelve task skills — akcli-circuit-design (core mechanics), akcli-schematic-authoring, akcli-schematic-review, akcli-circuit-debug, akcli-altium-interop, akcli-parts-sourcing, akcli-jlcpcb-capabilities, akcli-design-calc (60 standards-cited engineering calculators), akcli-setup (environment probe/repair via `akcli doctor`), akcli-datasheet-facts (audited PDF-pinned facts extraction), akcli-deep-review (LLM candidates gated by `review validate`), and akcli-release-gating (preflight manifest + calibrated blocking policy). KiCad is the writable target (files); Altium .SchDoc/.SchLib/.PcbDoc are read/imported into the same model, with an optional Windows live bridge to a running Altium instance — it is not a symmetric Altium↔KiCad converter.
defaultPrompt: Read main.SchDoc and summarize the components, rails, and connectors.
Run a severity-ranked design review of board.kicad_sch and report the findings.
Draw a 3V3 LDO regulator block into board.kicad_sch, then verify the netlist.
Diff v1.SchDoc against v2.SchDoc and tell me what regressed.
